questionHtml: Briefly explain the two differences in the application of Prim’s and Kruskal’s algorithms for finding a minimum spanning tree in a weighted connected graph.
markschemeHtml:
In Prim’s algorithm you start at a particular (given) vertex, whereas in Kruskal’s you start with the smallest edge.        A1

In Prim’s as smallest edges are added (never creating a circuit) the created graph always remains connected, whereas in Kruskal’s this requirement to always be connected is not necessary.        A1

[2 marks]

meta.title: Briefly explain the two differences in the application of Prim’s and Kruskal’s algorithms for finding a minimum spanning tree in a weighted connected graph.
